label: nondecomp
headline_statement: Refuted: the allowed observable law with \(p_0=p_1=1/2\), \(q_{1\mid0}=1/4\), \(q_{1\mid1}=3/4\), \(r_{0,0}=2/5\), \(r_{1,0}=3/5\), \(r_{0,1}=3/10\), and \(r_{1,1}=7/10\) satisfies \(\kappa(P)=3/10\neq0\) and \(q_{\cdot\mid1}\neq q_{\cdot\mid0}\), but for every \(0\le\lambda\le3/10\) the shared-\(U\) interval and the per-mediator box coincide, \(I(\lambda)=B_{\mathrm{box}}(\lambda)=[-\lambda,\lambda]\); hence no positive \(\lambda_0\) can make the inclusion strict against \(B_{\mathrm{box}}\).
witness_construction_if_refuted: Take \(p_0=p_1=1/2\), \(q_{1\mid0}=1/4\), \(q_{0\mid0}=3/4\), \(q_{1\mid1}=3/4\), \(q_{0\mid1}=1/4\), \(r_{0,0}=2/5\), \(r_{1,0}=3/5\), \(r_{0,1}=3/10\), and \(r_{1,1}=7/10\). Then \(q_{\cdot\mid1}\neq q_{\cdot\mid0}\) and \(\kappa(P)=3/10\neq0\). A two-point latent completion with \(w_0(a)=1\), \(w_1(b)=1\), \(\pi(a)=\pi(b)=1/2\), and exclusion-corner means \(\varphi(m,0,a)=\varphi(m,1,a)=r_{0,m}\), \(\varphi(m,0,b)=\varphi(m,1,b)=r_{1,m}\) satisfies all stated assumptions. For every \(0\le\lambda\le3/10\) and every \(t\in[-\lambda,\lambda]\), set \(\varphi(m,0,a)=r_{0,m}\), \(\varphi(m,1,a)=r_{0,m}+t\), \(\varphi(m,1,b)=r_{1,m}\), and \(\varphi(m,0,b)=r_{1,m}-t\). This keeps compatibility and the leak bound and attains \(\theta=t\), while the leak-average identity forces both \(I(\lambda)\) and \(B_{\mathrm{box}}(\lambda)\) inside \([-\lambda,\lambda]\). Thus \(I(\lambda)=B_{\mathrm{box}}(\lambda)=[-\lambda,\lambda]\), refuting the conjectured strict inclusion against the per-mediator box.
